content.text: SSO and SCIM are the two pillars of enterprise identity management for Claude.

replaces password-based Claude logins with your organisation's existing identity provider — users authenticate once against Okta, Azure AD, or any SAML 2.0 IdP and gain access without a separate Claude password.

automates user provisioning and deprovisioning so that onboarding a new employee or revoking a departed one in the IdP instantly propagates to Claude — no manual Admin API calls or Console clicks required.
Both are Enterprise plan features configured from the Anthropic Console under Organization Settings → Security.
. When a user navigates to claude.ai, Claude detects the organisation domain, builds a SAML

, and redirects the browser to the IdP login page. After the user authenticates (password, MFA, hardware key — whatever your IdP enforces), the IdP signs a SAML assertion and POSTs it back to Claude's
. Claude validates the signature against the IdP's certificate, matches or creates a user account, and establishes a session.
Create the Claude app in your IdP
into the IdP: ACS URL and Entity ID are shown in Console → Security → SSO.
(or metadata URL) from the IdP app into the Console SSO form.
— after enforcement all org members must authenticate via SSO; password login is blocked.

# Okta SAML app Attribute Statements
# Name                     Value
# NameID (email format)    user.email
firstName:                  user.firstName
lastName:                   user.lastName
department:                 user.department    # optional — used for workspace auto-assign rules

IdP signing certificates expire (typically every 3–5 years). Set a calendar reminder before expiry and update the Console's IdP certificate before the old one expires. Failure to rotate = complete SSO lockout for all users.

for users whose SAML assertion contains a valid email in the organisation's domain — they never need a manual invitation. JIT provisioning is enabled by default when SSO enforcement is active. You can disable it in Console → Security → SSO → Provisioning if you prefer to pre-provision via SCIM instead.
Any IdP user in the org domain
Broad rollouts where all employees should have access
Only users explicitly provisioned by SCIM
Regulated orgs that need explicit entitlement records

attrs.alt: SCIM 2.0 user lifecycle: IdP triggers SCIM API calls that update the Claude org

content.text: SCIM extends SSO by managing the full user lifecycle programmatically. The IdP acts as the source of truth — changes there propagate to Claude within seconds. SCIM removes the window between an employee leaving the company and their Claude access being revoked.

Push IdP groups to Claude workspaces by configuring
in your IdP SCIM app. The group name must match a Claude workspace
exactly (case-sensitive). Members of the IdP group are automatically added to the corresponding Claude workspace when the group is pushed.
"Engineering" and "engineering" are treated as different workspaces. Establish a naming convention before enabling group push and document it in your IdP app configuration.

whenever a team member with access to it leaves. Generate a new one in Console → Security → SCIM and update the IdP immediately.
— SSO shifts authentication to the IdP, so Claude's security now depends on the IdP's authentication strength.

call and that the member disappears from the Claude Console within 30 seconds.

The steps below walk through configuring a new SAML 2.0 application in Okta that targets Claude as the service provider.

Okta Admin Console → Applications → Create App Integration
  → SAML 2.0
  → App name: "Claude"
  → Logo: optional (upload from anthropic.com)

Single sign-on URL (ACS):  https://claude.ai/saml/acs/<your-org-id>
Audience URI (Entity ID):   https://claude.ai/saml/<your-org-id>
Name ID format:             EmailAddress
Application username:       Email
Copy both values from Console → Organization Settings → Security → SSO before filling in Okta.

Name         Name format   Value
firstName    Unspecified   user.firstName
lastName     Unspecified   user.lastName
email        Unspecified   user.email
Step 4 — Upload IdP metadata to Console
Download the Okta metadata XML from the SAML app's
link. Paste the raw XML (or the metadata URL) into Console → Security → SSO → IdP Metadata field. Claude validates the certificate automatically.
Before SSO can be enforced, you must verify ownership of your organisation's email domain via a DNS TXT record. Console shows the required TXT value; propagation takes up to 48 hours.
Azure AD uses slightly different terminology. The process mirrors Okta but the UI paths differ.

Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → Enterprise Applications
  → New application → Create your own application
  → Integrate any other application you don't find in the gallery
  → Set up single sign on → SAML
In the Basic SAML Configuration panel:

Identifier (Entity ID):       https://claude.ai/saml/<your-org-id>
Reply URL (ACS URL):          https://claude.ai/saml/acs/<your-org-id>
Sign on URL:                  https://claude.ai
Relay State:                  (leave blank)

Claim name                         Source attribute
http://schemas.../emailaddress     user.mail
firstName                          user.givenname
lastName                           user.surname

To pass group membership as a SAML attribute for workspace auto-assignment, add a Group Claim in the Attributes & Claims section and set the source to "Groups assigned to the application". Filter to security groups to avoid token size limits.
After enabling the Okta SAML app, enable
on the same app to activate SCIM.

Okta → Applications → Claude → Provisioning tab
  → Configure API Integration
  SCIM connector base URL:  https://api.anthropic.com/scim/v2
  Authentication Mode:      HTTP Header
  Authorization:            Bearer <scim-token-from-console>

  → Supported provisioning actions:
    [x] Push New Users
    [x] Push Profile Updates
    [x] Push Groups
    [x] Deactivate Users
request. A successful 200 response confirms connectivity.
Okta maps its internal user profile attributes to SCIM schema attributes. The defaults cover most scenarios:

Okta attribute          SCIM attribute
login                   userName
firstName               name.givenName
lastName                name.familyName
email                   emails[type eq "work"].value
department              urn:ietf:...:enterprise:2.0:User:department
To send org role via SCIM, add a custom Okta user attribute (e.g.
) and map it to the SCIM
. Enable it in the Claude Enterprise App → Provisioning tab.

Provisioning Mode: Automatic
Admin Credentials:
  Tenant URL:         https://api.anthropic.com/scim/v2
  Secret Token:       <scim-token-from-console>

Mappings:
  Provision Azure Active Directory Users  → enabled
  Provision Azure Active Directory Groups → enabled (for workspace sync)
Azure runs the SCIM sync cycle every 20–40 minutes by default. For immediate deprovisioning, use the
feature (search for the user → Provision) or trigger a sync via the Azure Portal or Microsoft Graph API.
Log in with a user who exists in the IdP — should land on claude.ai authenticated.
Use a new user not previously in Claude — should be auto-created on first SSO login.
Verify first name, last name, and email appear correctly in the Claude Console member list.
If sending role via SAML attribute, confirm the user's org role in Console matches what the IdP sent.
Confirm SLO (single logout) works if your IdP supports it — logging out of one app should invalidate the IdP session.
Add a test user to the IdP SCIM app — confirm they appear in Console → Members within 2 minutes.
Change the test user's last name in the IdP — confirm it updates in Claude.
Remove the test user from the IdP SCIM app — confirm they disappear from Console Members and can no longer log in to Claude.
Add the test user to an IdP group that matches a Claude workspace name — confirm they appear in that workspace's member list.
Generate or obtain the new IdP certificate (your IdP admin does this).

content: Update Console → Security → SSO → IdP Metadata with the new metadata XML that contains the new certificate.

content.text: Verify SSO still works with a test login before the old certificate expires.
Confirm in Console that the certificate fingerprint matches the new cert.
In Console → Security → SCIM, click Generate New Token.
Immediately update the IdP's SCIM app Secret Token / Authorization header with the new value.
Trigger a manual SCIM sync and confirm it succeeds.
Revoke the old token in the Console (it is invalidated as soon as a new one is generated).
If SSO goes down (IdP outage, certificate failure), org admins who set up a recovery account with
before enabling SSO enforcement can still access Console. Keep at least one admin account with a recovery email and password documented in your runbook.
Once enforcement is enabled, all members including admins must log in via SSO. The only bypass is the recovery account. Test recovery access before enabling enforcement in production.

SAML 2.0 SSO delegates authentication to your IdP, enforces your existing MFA policy, and eliminates Claude-specific passwords. SCIM 2.0 closes the lifecycle gap by propagating user additions, role changes, and removals from the IdP to Claude in near-real-time. Together they reduce administrative overhead to near zero: new employees get Claude access the moment they're onboarded in the IdP; departed employees lose access the moment they're offboarded — no manual intervention needed.
